steps.title: Translate the percent increase to a growth factor
steps.content: Use the exponential pattern directly. The initial count is $35$, and a $180\%$ increase means after one hour the count is $35+1.80(35)=98$. Use the values at $t=0$ and $t=1$ directly.

steps.title: Build the exponential model
steps.content: In $C=a(b)^t$, the starting amount is $a=35$. The hourly multiplier is $b=1+1.80=2.80$, so the model is $$C=35(2.8)^t$$ Choice D — $C=35{ \left(2.8\right)}^{t}$ has both the correct initial value and the correct hourly growth factor.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A) $C=35(1.18)^{t}$ — corresponds to a $18\%$ hourly increase. Eliminated.
  • B) $C=35(1.8)^{t}$ — corresponds to an $80\%$ increase per hour (forgets to add $1$). Eliminated.
  • C) $C=35(18)^{t}$ — corresponds to a $1700\%$ increase per hour. Eliminated.
  • D) $C=35(2.8)^{t}$ — matches the $180\%$ hourly increase.
That matches Choice D — $C=35{ \left(2.8\right)}^{t}$.
